summary.intent: Fix issue where copying a URL with a pipeline run parameter didn't show the full build chain (specifically build-fbc children) when accessed with default 24h time filter, even though searching for the run showed the complete chain.
summary.outcome: Implemented direct-link time filter bypass using fetch_single_run() and fetch_chain_nodes() in main.py, and added auto-rollout annotation to Deployment. However, discovered that fetch_chain_nodes only traverses one level deep, missing grandchildren (build-fbc runs) in the pipeline chain.

repo: Build chain relationships encoded via art.openshift.io/parent-pipelinerun label and art.openshift.io/triggered-* annotations
Pipeline structure: build-layered-products → olm-bundle-konflux → build-fbc-* (multi-level chain)
ImageStream auto-rollout requires image.openshift.io/triggers annotation on Deployment

code.finding: fetch_chain_nodes function only fetches direct children/parent, missing grandchildren in multi-level pipeline chains

workflow: Need to test with different time filters and direct URL access to catch chain traversal edge cases

friction: Initial fix didn't account for multi-level chain traversal
Single-level traversal in fetch_chain_nodes only revealed after testing with actual multi-level pipeline chain
open_items: Rewrite fetch_chain_nodes to do breadth-first recursive traversal of entire pipeline chain instead of stopping at one level
